codeReview.summary: The fix removes the earlier onboarding auto-success backdoor and resolves the immediate HomeAccessStateTests compile blocker, but it still relies on a new test-only readiness shortcut and leaves the previously flagged HomeAccessState test seam deficiency unresolved.

issues.description: When `--seed-test-home` is present and the HomeKit adapter returns no homes, `checkReadiness()` sets `.readyTesting` instead of surfacing `.noHomeConfigured`. This is still a test-only success shortcut: the UI tests can reach the post-blocker path without proving a legitimate readiness result from controllable HomeKit inputs, so the fix does not fully satisfy the requirement to remove onboarding success backdoors.

`HomeAccessMockAdapter.requestAuthorization()` still ignores the actor-configured `_requestAuthorizationResult` and always returns an authorized status. The prior scrutiny failure specifically called out that denied-after-request behavior could not be driven through the real `checkReadiness()`/`startHomeAccessFlow()` path; this fix only removes an unavailable `HMHome()` constructor and leaves that controllability gap intact.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The swiftui-feature-worker handoff claims the onboarding tests now prove a legitimate visible completion path after removing the success backdoor, but the code still depends on a seeded-data readiness shortcut (`readyTesting`) rather than real readiness results from the adapter path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `2026-04-09T22-32-10-493Z__dawnloop-onboarding-success-flow-validator-fix__eb7d3507-060b-4b43-a0de-9568776e356b.json` says `--simulate-home-ready` was added and the backdoor removed; current `DawnLoopApp/Onboarding/HomeAccessService.swift:188-194` instead uses `--seed-test-home` to force `.readyTesting` when `fetchHomes()` returns empty, and `DawnLoopUITests/OnboardingFlowTests.swift:16-20` depends on that seeded shortcut in every test.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo now has an undocumented testing convention where seeded SwiftData homes intentionally bypass the HomeKit readiness blocker by mapping empty adapter homes to `.readyTesting`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `DawnLoopApp/App/DawnLoopApp.swift:141-194` seeds `HomeReference`/`AccessoryReference` records for `--seed-test-home`, while `DawnLoopApp/Onboarding/HomeAccessService.swift:188-194` converts empty HomeKit homes into `.readyTesting`. AGENTS.md covers reset hooks not auto-completing flows, but it does not describe this seeded-ready exception or how workers should reason about it.

summary: Reviewed the fix feature transcript, handoff, current code, prior failed review, and both relevant diffs. The commit fixes the HMHome constructor compile issue and removes the earlier reset-driven auto-completion path, but it still fails scrutiny because onboarding success is now gated by a different test-only readiness shortcut (`readyTesting` on seeded homes) and the previously reported HomeAccessState authorization seam remains unresolved.
